title: Basic Searching Concepts
description:
Basic Searching Concepts

Basic Searching Concepts

Searching locates features by finding the area of the search window

to which the model is the most similar.  The figure below shows a model and a search window, and the areas within the search window that are most similar to the model.  A model similar to that shown below might be used to search for a feature such as a fiducial mark on a printed circuit board.  A robot could then use the position data returned by the search function to find the location of the board for placing components or for positioning the board itself.
